exploration_log.aha_moment: The duplicate-method check already existed in parseHttpMethodAndPath() but the task specifically wants it in parseMethodAnnotation() — adding it there as an early guard makes the intent explicit.

key_decisions.decision: Add early guard in parseMethodAnnotation() while keeping existing check in parseHttpMethodAndPath()
key_decisions.reasoning: Belt-and-suspenders approach ensures that any future call paths to parseHttpMethodAndPath() also validate; the guard in parseMethodAnnotation() fulfills the task requirement

queries.query_text: add duplicate HTTP method annotation check in parseMethodAnnotation in RequestFactory.java throwing IllegalArgumentException

solve_notes: Added a static `HTTP_METHOD_ANNOTATIONS` set and an early guard at the top of `parseMethodAnnotation()` that throws `IllegalArgumentException` (via `methodError()`) when a second HTTP method annotation is encountered. The existing check in `parseHttpMethodAndPath()` was kept as defense-in-depth.
task_text:
The `RequestFactory.Builder` in `RequestFactory.java` processes method
annotations sequentially but does not check for duplicate HTTP method
annotations (e.g., a method annotated with both `@GET` and `@POST`).
The second annotation silently overwrites the first. Add a check in
`parseMethodAnnotation()` that throws `IllegalArgumentException` when
multiple HTTP method annotations are present.

thrash_preventing_defs.name: parseHttpMethodAndPath
thrash_preventing_defs.reason: read: seeing this method upfront shows where the existing duplicate check lives, preventing an agent from thinking parseMethodAnnotation() has no related code and placing the guard incorrectly

thrash_preventing_defs.name: methodError
thrash_preventing_defs.reason: read: confirms methodError() throws IllegalArgumentException, so no manual throw new IllegalArgumentException() is needed

tier_difference_reasoning: The minimum set is just the edited method and constant. The thrash_preventing set adds parseHttpMethodAndPath() (so the agent sees the existing guard and doesn't duplicate it incorrectly) and methodError() in Utils.java (so the agent uses the established error-throwing helper rather than writing its own exception throw).
